perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party **is not required** to inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the receiving party is not required to inform associates of confidential nature, this directly contradicts the original obligation that they 'shall inform'. This creates uncertainty about the associates' responsibility to maintain confidentiality, potentially leading to breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the receiving party is 'not required' to inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states the receiving party 'shall inform' its Associates. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the obligation to inform associates about the confidential nature of the information, and aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall **not** be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the receiving party's responsibility for breaches by its associates directly contradicts the original agreement. This creates uncertainty on who is liable when associates disclose confidential information, weakening the agreement's protective measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states the receiving party is 'not' responsible for breaches by its Associates. The contradicted text states that the receiving party 'shall be responsible' for such breaches. This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. The perturbation explanation confirms that this weakens the agreement's protective measures, solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include **no degree of care** that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the obligation to include 'no degree of care', a contradiction is created where both parties are not obligated to keep any degree of care to protect confidentiatlity of the other party. It undermines the intention of a confidentiality agreement which is to protect the information shared. The original text obligated them to protect at least the same degree of care, as they protect the own.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text states that each party will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of the other party's information, including at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information. The changed text states the opposite, saying the measures will include 'no degree of care'. This creates a direct conflict, which makes it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as **publicly available** information.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the agreement and its terms are publicly available creates an in-text contradiction with the original text. The modification eliminates the confidentiality surrounding the agreement, allowing free disclosure to any third party, directly opposing the initial provision for strict confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party. 3. Required Disclosure . In the event that a receiving party or its Associates is or becomes legally compelled under applicable law, regulation or securities exchange listing agreement, or by a competent governmental, administrative or regulatory authority or in a proceeding before a court, arbitrator or administrative agency, whether or not as a result of a Transaction to which the Company has consented, to disclose any portion of the Confidential Information of a disclosing party, that discussions or negotiations between the parties hereto are taking place or have taken place, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to a Transaction, including the status thereof, the receiving party will, and will direct its Associates to, provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice (unless prohibited by law) of such legal compulsion, and shall delay disclosure, if and to the extent permitted or practicable, until the disclaiming party has had an opportunity to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or to waive compliance by the receiving party and/or its Associates with the relevant provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement and its terms are 'publicly available information,' while the contradicted text explicitly states that the same information 'shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence...and shall not be disclosed to any third party.' This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction, as something cannot be simultaneously public and confidential. The explanation is correct, this is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties and shall continue for a period of eighteen (18) months following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall **not** survive any termination of discussions between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the survival clause after termination directly contradicts the original intention for confidentiality to last 18 months post-termination. With this change, once discussions end, all confidentiality obligations cease, creating risk around shared confidential information during discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties and shall continue for a period of eighteen (18) months following the date of this Agreement. 12. Attorney-Client Privilege . To the extent that any Confidential Information of the disclosing party may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, the parties understand and agree that they have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is their desire, intention and mutual understanding that the sharing of such material is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such material or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are identical, both stating that confidentiality survives termination for 18 months. The changed text explicitly states that confidentiality does *not* survive termination, directly contradicting the original intention as well as the duplicated statement elsewhere in the document. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as one clause affirms a condition while another denies it, making enforcement unclear.
